steps.title: Check current kepler deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kepler version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kepler -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kepler
helm list -n kepler 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kepler installation.

steps.title: Review kepler configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kepler configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kepler -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kepler
kubectl get configmap -n kepler -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kepler
```
Currently, we are trying to run kepler in a Kubernetes v1.25.9 cluster and ran into a segmentation violation trying to start the exporter.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1.

steps.title: Apply the fix for SIGSEGV on Ubuntu 20
steps.description:
Fix #636

The cgroup v1 stat had `IgnoreNotExist`, but if the cgroup does not exist if returns an empty structure, which leads to segmentation fault.
```yaml
I0418 12:10:46.309045       1 gpu.go:43] Failed to init nvml, err: could not init nvml: error opening libnvidia-ml.so.1: libnvidia-ml.so.1: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory
I0418 12:10:46.312663       1 exporter.go:149] Kepler running on version: d1bfdc4
I0418 12:10:46.312718       1 config.go:169] using gCgroup ID in the BPF program: true
I0418 12:10:46.313033       1 config.go:170] kernel version: 5.4
I0418 12:10:46.313353       1 rapl_msr_util.go:129] failed to open path /dev/cpu/0/msr: no such file or directory
I0418 12:10:46.313684       1 power.go:64] Not able to
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kepler -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kepler
kubectl get events -n kepler --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "SIGSEGV on Ubuntu 20" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Fix #636

The cgroup v1 stat had `IgnoreNotExist`, but if the cgroup does not exist if returns an empty structure, which leads to segmentation fault.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kepler installed or the issue environment reproducible.
